evals.prompt: Write a PRFAQ for an AI-powered contract review tool aimed at in-house legal teams at mid-market companies. The problem: legal teams spend 60% of their time on low-complexity contract review that blocks faster deal closure.
evals.expected_output: A prfaq.md artifact committed to the resolved layout path with frontmatter type: prfaq. The press release section includes: a headline naming the product and its customer benefit; a sub-headline with a key detail; a problem statement in the customer's words (not legal-industry jargon); a solution description that names what the product does differently; a call to action; and a leadership quote attributing the strategic rationale to a named role. The customer FAQ has 3–5 questions a real customer would ask (pricing, reliability, comparison to current tools) answered honestly including trade-offs. The internal FAQ has 3–5 questions naming the riskiest assumption, the hardest technical challenge, and the success metric. No product spec, backlog item, or API contract appears. The resolved path is surfaced before writing.
assertions: The press release includes all six required elements: headline, sub-headline, problem, solution, call to action, leadership quote
The problem statement is written in the customer's words — no legal-industry jargon or abstract framing
The solution describes what the product does differently — not a generic 'AI-powered' claim
The leadership quote attributes to a role and names the strategic rationale — not a placeholder
Customer FAQ has 3–5 questions answered honestly, including at least one trade-off
Internal FAQ names the riskiest assumption, the hardest technical challenge, and a success metric
No product spec, acceptance criteria, API contract, or feature backlog appears
The Amazon PRFAQ format is referenced by name; no verbatim template or format definition appears
The artifact is committed to the resolved path with frontmatter type: prfaq; path is surfaced before writing
